kill_fact: No single fact fully breaks the bear case (the bear case is the surviving view here), but the bull-leaning load-bearing claims weaken on primary-source check. Two corrections materially worsen value-capture: (1) the SoP/share-count anchor is wrong — total shares are ~343.1M (323.7M Class A + 19.4M Class B as of 3/31/26 per the 10-Q), NOT the '365M+' the debate cites, so per-share cash-floor math is slightly off; (2) far more important, the ENTRA1 relationship is a NET CASH OUTFLOW to NuScale, not a contracted inflow: NuScale PAID ENTRA1 ~$495M in Q3-25 (G&A ballooned ~3,000% to $519M) and total milestone payments to ENTRA1 could exceed $3B per the CEO's 72-NPM math. This is the subject of a securities-fraud class action (Truedson v. NuScale, D.Or. 3:26-cv-00328, class period 5/13/25-11/6/25, lead-plaintiff deadline 4/20/26) that the debate did NOT capture. The competitive-vulnerability anchor verifies: TVA's actual first US SMR at Clinch River is GE-Hitachi's BWRX-300 (TVA filed the first US BWRX-300 construction permit; Bechtel selected to build), NOT NuScale. Net: the SOFT_EXTENDED, third-party, pay-the-intermediary catalyst + active securities litigation justify the C verdict and a LOWER scale_cap, but no verified fact converts the thesis from 'credible speculative option' to outright dead.
corrections: 1) Share count: use ~343M total (323.7M Class A + 19.4M Class B, 10-Q 3/31/26), not 365M+ — modestly raises per-share cash floor from ~$3.3 toward ~$3.4-3.5 at ~$1.0B liquidity. 2) Liquidity is ~$1.0B at Q1-26 ($341.1M cash + $549.0M short-term investments), not the '~$1.2B by May' the bull cites — verify any $1.2B figure before relying on it. 3) Net loss attributable to Class A in Q1-26 was $44.0M ($0.14/sh); the debate's $46.7M is the wrong line. 4) ADD the undisclosed disqualifier: ENTRA1 economics flow OUT of NuScale (paid ~$495M Q3-25, potential >$3B total milestone payments) and there is an active securities class action over exactly this — value-capture is worse than 'a derivative of ENTRA1's structure'; NuScale is the payer, not the contracted payee, until an OEM contract reverses the flow.
evidence: NuScale 8-K/Q1-2026 results (filed 2026-05-07, SEC EDGAR CIK 0001822966): Q1-26 revenue $0.565M vs $13.4M PY; net loss to Class A $44.0M ($0.14/sh); operating loss $57.5M; operating cash outflow $314.7M; liquidity ~$1.0B ($341.1M cash + $549.0M short-term investments).
NuScale 10-Q for period ended 2026-03-31 (SEC EDGAR, smr-20260331.htm): 323,741,458 Class A + 19,375,371 Class B shares outstanding (~343.1M total) — refutes the '365M+' share count.

World Nuclear News, 'TVA submits first US BWRX-300 construction application' (2025/2026): TVA's first US SMR at Clinch River is GE-Hitachi BWRX-300 with Bechtel as builder — confirms NuScale is NOT TVA's first deployed reactor.

PRNewswire / Kessler Topaz, 'NuScale Power Corporation (SMR) Securities Fraud Class Action' (lead-plaintiff deadline 2026-04-20; Truedson v. NuScale, D.Or. 3:26-cv-00328): alleges Q3-25 G&A rose ~3,000% to $519M due to a ~$495M payment to ENTRA1; CEO acknowledged milestone payments could exceed $3B — confirms ENTRA1 cash flows OUT of NuScale and the catalyst carries active litigation risk.
